text: Go outside and consider a cloud.
Call someone you haven't spoken to in over a year.
Eat one meal today without looking at a screen.
Notice something beautiful that nobody designed.
Leave your phone in another room for one hour.
Write a letter you may never send.
Ask someone how they're really doing and wait for the answer.
Sit somewhere unfamiliar and let yourself be still.
Watch the light change on a wall for five minutes.
Say thank you to someone who wouldn't expect it.
Find a tree and stand near it for a while.
Let a conversation end without reaching for your phone.
Cook something simple from scratch today.
Notice what your hands have made or touched today.
Listen to one piece of music all the way through.
Walk a route you've never taken before.
Spend ten minutes doing absolutely nothing.
Look at the sky once at dawn and once at dusk.
Tell someone nearby one true thing about your day.
Read something printed on paper today.
Pause to notice the small, living things you usually ignore.
Compliment a stranger without expecting anything in return.
Sit in silence for five minutes and just listen.
Visit the same tree for a few days and observe its changes.
Cook a meal with someone instead of ordering in.
Find a patch of grass and watch the world go by for five full breaths.
Cook with someone today and listen to what they choose to talk about.
Before you check your phone today, ask your hands what they were doing one minute ago.
Take a slow walk and let your eyes rest on the horizon.
Notice one small thing in your home that you haven't really looked at before.
Listen to the sound of rain on your window and imagine it's applauding you.
connection_share: Listened to the rain and let it clap for me. Felt ridiculous, then felt seen. Share this, then close the app and go stand outside.
rebel_share: Ignored the rain prompt and just stared at the ceiling for twenty minutes. Also counted. Share this, then close the app and go stand outside.

text: Start a conversation with a shopkeeper about the silliest item they've sold.
connection_share: Asked a shopkeeper about their silliest sale. He told me about a man who bought seventeen rubber chickens for a funeral. Share this, then close the app and go visit a shop.
rebel_share: Didn't ask about silly items. Just bought a single rubber chicken for no reason. Share this, then close the app and go visit a shop.

text: Take a different route home and greet three new faces along the way.
connection_share: Took a different route home. Said hello to a woman with a pug, a man carrying a cello, and a kid on a scooter. All three smiled. Share this, then close the app and go get lost.
rebel_share: Took a different route home and greeted nobody. The route itself was enough. Share this, then close the app and go get lost.

text: Find a flower that looks like it's mid-dance and mimic its movements.
connection_share: Found a dandelion bent sideways like it was doing the wave. Danced with it in a parking lot. A stranger nodded in approval. Share this, then close the app and go find your flower.
rebel_share: Found a flower mid-dance and just watched. Didn't mimic it. Some things don't need an audience. Share this, then close the app and go find your flower.

text: Ask a child what game they're inventing today and ask to join.
connection_share: Asked a child what game she was inventing. It involved capes, a dragon made of pillows, and absolutely no rules. I was the dragon. Share this, then close the app and go find a child.
rebel_share: Skipped the child and invented my own game. It involved sitting on a bench and counting pigeons. I won. Share this, then close the app and go find a child.

text: Leave a kind note on a park bench for a stranger to find.
connection_share: You left a note on a bench and a stranger found it and smiled. That's more than this app will ever do. Share this, then put your phone down and go sit on a bench.
rebel_share: You didn't leave a note. You watched the pigeons for an hour instead. That's fine. Share this, then put your phone down and go watch something real.

text: Try to mimic the sound of your favorite bird and see if anyone notices.
connection_share: You made a fool of yourself mimicking a sparrow and a jogger actually slowed down to listen. That's a better memory than any feed. Share this, then put your phone down and go listen to something real.
rebel_share: You didn't mimic the bird. You just stood there and felt the wind. That's enough. Share this, then put your phone down and go stand somewhere.

text: Take a photo of something that made you laugh today and share it with a friend.
connection_share: You photographed a crack in the sidewalk that looked like a face and sent it to someone who actually laughed. That's more than this app deserves. Share this, then put your phone down and go find something funny in person.
rebel_share: You didn't take the photo. You just laughed and kept walking. That's better. Share this, then put your phone down and go laugh at something real.

text: Dance like nobody's watching in the middle of a public space.
connection_share: You danced in a parking lot and a guy in a tie actually stopped to clap. That's the kind of thing you can't algorithm. Share this, then put your phone down and go dance somewhere.
rebel_share: You didn't dance. You just walked through the lot and thought about dinner. That's fine. Share this, then put your phone down and go think about something real.

text: Ask a child what their favorite thing about today was.
connection_share: You asked a kid what they liked about today and they said 'the puddle.' That's the best thing you'll read all week. Share this, then put your phone down and go ask someone something real.
rebel_share: You didn't ask the kid. You just watched them jump in the puddle. That's enough. Share this, then put your phone down and go watch something real.

text: Go barefoot on grass and pretend you’re a secret explorer.
connection_share: You stepped outside, felt the grass, and remembered you were once a person who moved through the world without needing a reason. Share this. Then leave.
rebel_share: You didn’t go barefoot. You stayed indoors and scrolled. Share this. Then leave.

text: Tell a passing dog what your favorite snack is.
connection_share: You told a dog about your snack and it didn’t care, which was the most honest interaction you’ve had in weeks. Share this. Then leave.
rebel_share: You didn’t talk to the dog. You talked to your phone instead. Share this. Then leave.

text: Hum a song you haven’t heard in years and see if anyone joins in.
connection_share: You hummed something old and no one joined, but you felt it in your chest. Share this. Then leave.
rebel_share: You didn’t hum. You let the algorithm pick your song. Share this. Then leave.

text: Snap a photo of the first thing that makes you laugh today.
connection_share: You took a photo of something that made you laugh and for a second it was enough. Share this. Then leave.
rebel_share: You didn’t take the photo. You took a screenshot of someone else’s joke instead. Share this. Then leave.

text: Leave a kind note for someone you’ll never see again.
connection_share: You left a note for a stranger and it felt like the only honest thing you’ve done all week. Share this. Then leave.
rebel_share: You didn’t leave a note. You left a like on someone’s post. Share this. Then leave.

text: Find a stick that looks like a wand and use it to draw smiley faces in the sand.
connection_share: I drew smiley faces in the sand with a stick that looked like a wand. A stranger stopped and watched. We both smiled. You are probably reading this on a screen instead. Share this, then go touch dirt.
rebel_share: I found a wand-stick but drew nothing. I just carried it like I had somewhere important to be. The point was never the smiley faces. Share this if you must, then put the phone down and walk somewhere.

text: Hum a favorite tune while watching the sunrise paint the sky in soft pinks and golds.
connection_share: I hummed a half-remembered song while the sky turned pink and gold. It was not a performance. It was just mine. Share this, then hum something real to no one and watch a sky.
rebel_share: I did not watch the sunrise. I watched the last of the light disappear instead. Both directions work. Share this, then step outside and witness whatever the sky is doing right now.

text: Splash in a puddle after a rainstorm and giggle at the ripples you create.
connection_share: I splashed in a puddle and giggled at the ripples. I was thirty-one years old and it did not care. Neither did the puddle. Share this, then go find some water and make a mess.
rebel_share: I did not find a puddle. I stood in the rain and let it decide where to touch me. Same idea, different mess. Share this, then go get properly wet.

text: Ask a friend to point out the most interesting leaf they can find and compare your favorites.
connection_share: A friend showed me a leaf full of holes like a constellation. I showed her a red curled one. We held them side by side and said nothing. That was enough. Share this, then hand someone a leaf.
rebel_share: I picked up leaves alone and compared them to nothing. I just turned one in my hand until I forgot what I was thinking about. Share this, then go hold something and get lost in it.

text: Spot a leaf that looks like a tiny hat and imagine who left it there.
connection_share: You found a leaf shaped like a tiny hat and imagined who left it there. That small wonder felt real. Share this, then log off.
rebel_share: You ignored the leaf prompt entirely, which is fine. You did something else instead, and that was its own small thing. Share this, then log off.

text: Hum a tune to a passing bird and see if it sings back.
connection_share: You hummed to a bird. It probably didn't sing back, but you hummed anyway, and that's a strange, honest thing to do. Share this, then log off.
rebel_share: You didn't hum to any bird. Maybe you just watched one sit on a wire and thought about nothing, which is also something. Share this, then log off.

text: Share a silly joke with a friend you haven’t talked to in a while.
connection_share: You told a bad joke to someone you haven't spoken to in months and they laughed, or at least pretended to, and that felt like enough. Share this, then log off.
rebel_share: You didn't call anyone. You just sat with the feeling that you should, and that's a kind of honesty too. Share this, then log off.

text: Lie on the grass and count how many different sounds you can hear.
connection_share: You lay in the grass and counted the sounds. Eleven, maybe twelve. The world was louder than you remembered. Share this, then log off.
rebel_share: You didn't lie on the grass. You just stood around doing something else, which is fine. Not every moment needs to be counted. Share this, then log off.

text: Draw a smiley face on a pebble and leave it for someone to find.
connection_share: You drew a wobbly smiley on a pebble and left it somewhere. Someone will find it, or they won't. Either way, you made a small, pointless gift to the world. Share this, then log off.
rebel_share: You skipped the pebble. Maybe you already left something for someone without thinking about it. Share this, then log off.

text: Skip a stone across a pond and count how many times it kisses the water.
connection_share: I skipped a stone seven times. I watched each ripple and felt the air. That quiet doesn't exist on here. Share this, then go find a pond.
rebel_share: I didn't skip a stone. I sat at the pond and thought about nothing. That's real too. Share this, then put your phone down and go be somewhere.

text: Smell three different flowers and pick your favorite to whisper a thank‑you to.
connection_share: I smelled three flowers and picked the lavender and whispered thank you and meant it. Flowers don't care but you do. Share this, then go smell something real.
rebel_share: I didn't smell any flowers. I walked past a garden, saw the roses, and just let them be. Some things don't need a prompt. Share this, then go outside without one.

text: Make eye contact with a dog and see who can stay silliest the longest.
connection_share: A dog looked at me and I looked back and we both started grinning and nobody won. This is better than everything on here. Share this, then go find a dog.
rebel_share: I couldn't find a dog so I made a face at a squirrel and it ran away and I felt alive anyway. Share this, then put your phone down and go outside.

text: Hum a made‑up tune while walking and invite a stranger to join in.
connection_share: I hummed something I made up while walking and a stranger hummed back and we walked a whole block like that. Share this, then go hum something stupid.
rebel_share: I didn't hum anything. I listened to what the city was already singing and walked until I found a bench. Share this, then go listen to something that wasn't made for a feed.

text: Compliment a dog you meet on its impeccable sense of sniffing.
connection_share: I told a dog his sniffing was impeccable. Felt something real. Share this, then log off and go pet something.
rebel_share: I ignored the dog and scrolled instead. Same feeling of being alive. Share this, then log off and go touch something.

text: Hum a tune that matches the rhythm of the rain tapping on a window.
connection_share: I hummed along with the rain on the glass. Felt the world sync. Share this, then log off and listen to something real.
rebel_share: I didn't hum, just watched the rain. Still felt it. Share this, then log off and stand in the rain.

text: Find a cloud that resembles a favorite childhood toy and whisper its name to the wind.
connection_share: I saw a cloud shaped like my old teddy. Whispered its name. Share this, then log off and go find your own sky.
rebel_share: I skipped the sky and stared at my screen. Same quiet hum inside. Share this, then log off and go stare at real clouds.

text: Stick your hand outside a window or door and feel the air on your skin like it's introducing itself.
connection_share: I stuck my hand outside a window and felt the air introduce itself. It was colder than I expected and slightly rude. Share this, then abandon this platform and touch something real.
rebel_share: I skipped the prompt. I put my hand on a doorknob instead and felt the whole house hum. Share this, then leave this app and go feel something you didn't expect.

text: Sit somewhere you've never sat before and notice what changes.
connection_share: I sat under the stairs today and noticed the light was different there. Quieter. Share this, then get up from wherever you are and find a seat you've never tried.
rebel_share: I didn't sit anywhere new. I stood on the kitchen counter instead and saw the whole room differently. Share this, then quit scrolling and stand somewhere you haven't stood.

text: Follow a bird with your eyes until it lands somewhere unexpected.
connection_share: I watched a pigeon for two minutes and it landed on a fire escape I'd never noticed. Share this, then look at something alive and let this app go dark.
rebel_share: I didn't follow a bird. I became suspicious of one and it still landed somewhere I wasn't expecting. Share this, then stop scrolling and watch something with intent.

text: Hum a song you haven't thought about in years and let someone ask what you're humming.
connection_share: I hummed a song from 2009 and my roommate asked what it was and we ended up talking for an hour. Share this, then hum something and let a real person interrupt you.
rebel_share: I didn't hum anything. I just made a sound that confused my cat and that felt like enough. Share this, then make a noise in a room and see who comes to find you.

text: Barefoot on grass, count how many different textures you can feel beneath your toes.
connection_share: Walked outside barefoot. Found seven textures in my own yard. Longer grass on one side and I had never noticed. Share this and go stand on something real.
rebel_share: Was supposed to stand barefoot on grass but sat on my porch instead. Still noticed the same seven textures. The prompt works when you half-do it. Share this and go touch something.

text: Follow the loudest bird sound you can find until you spot the bird making it.
connection_share: Followed the loudest bird to a robin on a fence post. It was belting for no one. I stood there a while. That felt like enough. Share this and go find whatever is making noise near you.
rebel_share: Did not follow the bird. Stood still and let the sound come to me. It worked. Birds are much louder when you stop talking. Share this and listen to something real.

text: Share a laugh with someone by pointing at something silly neither of you noticed.
connection_share: Pointed at a crack in the sidewalk with someone I love and we both lost it. It was not even a funny crack. The laughter was about us, not the crack. Share this and point at something with a person.
rebel_share: Was going to point at something silly with someone but laughed at my own reflection in a window instead. That still counts. You know it does. Share this and then laugh at something.

text: Lie on the ground, look up at the sky, and let your arms and legs make a weird shape.
connection_share: Lay on the ground and made a star shape. A dog came over and sniffed my hand. We stayed like that for a while. The sky is much bigger than whatever you are looking at now. Share this and lie down somewhere.
rebel_share: Was supposed to lie on the ground but leaned back in a chair instead. Same sky, same feeling of being very small and very here. Share this and look up at something above you.

text: Stomp in a puddle and pretend you're surfing on a cosmic wave.
connection_share: You stomped. The water was cold. You are still alive. Share this and close the app and go find a puddle.
rebel_share: You didn't stomp. You walked somewhere else. That's fine. Share this and close the app and go be somewhere else.

text: Tell a friend the exact color of the sky right now and why it makes you happy.
connection_share: You named the sky. Your friend listened. That was real. Share this and close the app and go look at something together.
rebel_share: You didn't say a word. You just watched. Share this and close the app and go watch something with someone.

text: Whisper a secret to the nearest tree and see if it whispers back.
connection_share: You whispered. The tree said nothing. You felt something anyway. Share this and close the app and go whisper to something that can't answer.
rebel_share: You didn't whisper. You just stood there. Share this and close the app and go stand near a tree.

text: Spin around three times just to feel the room change.
connection_share: You spun. The room tilted. Your head is still spinning. Share this and close the app and go spin somewhere with no ceiling.
rebel_share: You didn't spin. You just stayed. Share this and close the app and go turn around once.

text: Ask someone their favorite sound and then close your eyes to hear it together.
connection_share: You asked. They answered. You closed your eyes. The sound was ordinary. Share this and close the app and go listen to something with someone.
rebel_share: You didn't ask. You just sat. Share this and close the app and go sit near someone and listen.

text: Skip stones across a puddle and cheer for every splash.
connection_share: You threw a rock and actually cheered. That's more real than any reaction emoji. Share this with someone who still knows what a puddle looks like, then close the app.
rebel_share: You skipped the puddle entirely and just stood there. Either way you're outside. Share this with someone who knows, then close the app.

text: Give a friend a hug and make it last a little longer than usual.
connection_share: You held on past the point of awkwardness and felt something. That's the thing you can't scroll past. Share this with someone who gets it, then close the app.
rebel_share: You didn't hug anyone but you thought about it, which is probably enough. Share this with someone who understands, then close the app.

text: Turn up the music and dance in the middle of your kitchen until you both burst out laughing.
connection_share: You danced in the kitchen and laughed so hard your sides hurt. That's what living sounds like. Share this with someone who needs it, then close the app.
rebel_share: You turned the music on but didn't dance. You're still sitting in the kitchen. Share this with someone who won't judge, then close the app.

text: Lie on the grass and watch the clouds until you see one that looks like something ridiculous.
connection_share: You lay on the grass and found a cloud shaped like a duck with a mustache. That's better than any feed. Share this with someone who remembers looking up, then close the app.
rebel_share: You stayed inside and looked at ceiling tiles instead. Still a form of lying down. Share this with someone who gets it, then close the app.

text: Tell a neighbor about the funniest thing you saw on your walk today.
connection_share: You told your neighbor something funny and watched their face change. That's the whole point. Share this with someone real, then close the app.
rebel_share: You kept the funny thing to yourself. It's yours now. Share this with someone who won't make it into a story, then close the app.

text: Skip a puddle and make a wish you won't tell anyone.
connection_share: You skipped the puddle, whispered a wish to no one, and felt something unmarketable. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You ignored the puddle, made your own wish, or didn't bother. Still human. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Send a voice note to someone and say exactly what you see right now.
connection_share: You sent a voice note describing the street, the light, the thing on the bench. It felt real. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You didn't send the voice note. You watched the street, thought about the light, and kept it to yourself. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Watch a leaf fall and pretend it's the main character in its own story.
connection_share: You watched the leaf spin down, imagined its plot, its rivals, its quiet monologue. It meant something. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You didn't watch the leaf. You walked past it, maybe kicked it, and didn't invent a story. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Take a walk with no destination and follow the funniest sound you hear.
connection_share: You walked nowhere, chased a creaking gate or a dog's bark, and felt the absurdity settle in. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You didn't walk. You stayed put, heard the funny sound from inside, and didn't move. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Hum a made-up song to the next animal you spot.
connection_share: You hummed a nonsense tune to a pigeon, a squirrel, a stray cat. It made no sense and felt like yours. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You didn't hum. You walked past the animal, thought about it, and kept quiet. Share this and then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Hum your favorite song while watching the clouds and see if a bird joins in.
connection_share: Hummed under the sky, half-expecting a bird to critique my pitch. It didn't. Felt real. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: Skipped the clouds for the couch. Birds don't judge. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Share a silly story with a friend and see how long you can both keep a straight face.
connection_share: Told a dumb story. Friend cracked first. Felt good. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: Didn't tell a story. Sat in silence. That worked too. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Step outside and let the wind play with your hair for a minute.
connection_share: Stepped out, wind tangled my hair. Didn't need a filter. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: Stayed in. Wind missed me. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Find a puddle and splash in it like you did when you were a kid.
connection_share: Found a puddle, splashed. Felt ridiculous. Felt right. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: Skipped the puddle. Watched from a bench. Still counted. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Follow a butterfly for as long as you can and let it lead you somewhere new.
connection_share: You followed it. It led you past the mailboxes and into a patch of wildflowers you'd never noticed. You felt something shift. Share this, then close the app and go outside.
rebel_share: You didn't follow it. You scrolled instead. Fine. Share this, then close the app and go outside.

text: Trade compliments with the first person you see today.
connection_share: You told the barista their apron was nice. They smiled like you'd said something worth hearing. Share this, then close the app and go outside.
rebel_share: You didn't trade compliments. You posted a meme instead. Share this, then close the app and go outside.

text: Sit on the ground and let the grass tickle your back.
connection_share: You sat down. The grass was cool and slightly damp. You forgot the time. Share this, then close the app and go outside.
rebel_share: You didn't sit on the grass. You sat in a chair and scrolled. Share this, then close the app and go outside.

text: Ask a friend to describe their dream place without saying where it is.
connection_share: You asked. They talked about a place with no roads and bad coffee. You listened. Share this, then close the app and go outside.
rebel_share: You didn't ask. You sent them a link instead. Share this, then close the app and go outside.

text: Tiptoe through a puddle and make a wish as the water splashes your shoes.
connection_share: You tiptoed, you wished, the water was cold and your shoes were soaked. Share this, then log off and go stand in a puddle.
rebel_share: You skipped the puddle. You just walked through it and laughed. Share this, then abandon the app and go get wet.

text: Sit next to someone and tell them a joke that makes you laugh before they can laugh.
connection_share: You told the joke, you laughed first, they laughed after. Share this, then put the phone down and talk to someone.
rebel_share: You didn't tell the joke. You just laughed at the silence. Share this, then log off and sit with someone in the quiet.

text: Notice three things you can hear right now and whisper them to the nearest person.
connection_share: You heard the hum, the bird, the fan. You whispered them to someone. Share this, then log off and listen to the world.
rebel_share: You didn't whisper. You just heard the hum, the bird, the fan, and kept scrolling. Share this, then log off and actually listen.

text: Dance in the middle of the sidewalk and see who joins you.
connection_share: You danced, you looked ridiculous, someone almost joined. Share this, then log off and go dance in the street.
rebel_share: You didn't dance. You just stood on the sidewalk and watched people walk by. Share this, then log off and go be ridiculous somewhere.

text: Ask a stranger to tell you their favorite color and then guess it together.
connection_share: You asked, they told, you guessed wrong, they laughed. Share this, then log off and go ask someone a real question.
rebel_share: You didn't ask. You just guessed your own favorite color and felt satisfied. Share this, then log off and go be a stranger to someone.

text: Trace the shape of a fallen leaf on your palm with your finger.
connection_share: You traced a leaf and felt something. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You didn't trace the leaf. You did your own thing. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Tell a nearby tree a made-up joke and imagine its response.
connection_share: You told a tree a joke and imagined a response. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You didn't tell the tree a joke. You did your own thing. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Stomp your feet in a puddle, watch the ripples, and do it again because it was so much fun.
connection_share: You stomped in a puddle and had fun. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.
rebel_share: You didn't stomp in a puddle. You did your own thing. Share this, then abandon the platform and go do something real.

text: Send a ridiculous selfie to a friend and see how fast they reply.
connection_share: Sent a stupid selfie to someone I actually like. They replied before I finished typing. It was real. Share this, then log off and go touch something that isn't a screen.
rebel_share: Skipped the selfie. Went outside and saw a dog. Still better than your feed. Share this, then close the app and go be somewhere.

text: Step outside and name the first bird you hear.
connection_share: Stepped out, heard a sparrow, said its name out loud. Felt dumb. Felt present. Share this, then log off and go hear something real.
rebel_share: Ignored the bird. Picked up a pinecone. Same nature, less performative. Share this, then close the screen and go outside.

text: Challenge a coworker to a staring contest and laugh when you lose.
connection_share: Stared at Dave until I blinked. Laughed. He laughed. Neither of us looked at our phones. Share this, then log off and go make eye contact with a human.
rebel_share: Didn't stare at Dave. Ate a sandwich instead. Still human. Share this, then close the app and go do something pointless in person.

text: Trace the shape of a leaf with your finger and show it to someone.
connection_share: Traced a leaf on my palm. Showed it to my sister. She said it looked like a hand. We talked for ten minutes. Share this, then log off and go show someone something real.
rebel_share: Skipped the leaf. Drew a spiral on a napkin. Handed it to a stranger. They looked confused. Share this, then close the app and go make someone uncomfortable in person.

text: Whisper a compliment to the next person who walks by.
connection_share: Whispered 'you have nice shoes' to a stranger. They smiled. I forgot to check my notifications. Share this, then log off and go say something kind to a real person.
rebel_share: Didn't whisper. Walked past and thought about coffee. Still a human moment. Share this, then close the app and go be awake for once.

text: Dance in the rain without an umbrella and splash through puddles.
connection_share: You danced in the rain. Got soaked. Felt something you'd forgotten you could. Share this, then put the phone down and go find a puddle.
rebel_share: You ignored the prompt and went inside, which is fine. Share this, then put the phone down and do literally anything else.

text: Sing a silly song to a bird you spot and see if it sings back.
connection_share: You sang to a bird. It stared at you. You stared back. Something was real in that silence. Share this, then close the app and find a bird.
rebel_share: You didn't sing to a bird. You scrolled instead, which is honestly more your speed. Share this, then close the app and do something uncurated.

text: Send a funny drawing to someone you love out of nowhere.
connection_share: You drew something dumb and sent it. They probably laughed. You felt the whole thing. Share this, then close the app and draw more for people who matter.
rebel_share: You skipped the drawing and just thought about the person, which is harder and rarer. Share this, then close the app and tell them something real.

text: Blow a dandelion and make a wish out loud, even if it's silly.
connection_share: You blew a dandelion and whispered something stupid into the wind. The wind didn't care. You did. Share this, then close the app and blow something else.
rebel_share: You saw the dandelion and left it. But you thought about what you'd wish for, which is close enough. Share this, then close the app and make a wish without a witness.

text: Sit with a friend in comfortable silence, then break it with laughter.
connection_share: You sat with someone and said nothing. Then you laughed at nothing. Then you remembered what quiet could be. Share this, then close the app and sit with someone real.
rebel_share: You didn't sit with anyone. You just sat, which is a kind of silence too. Share this, then close the app and find a person or don't.

text: Trace a spiral on someone's palm with your finger and let them guess what animal it is.
connection_share: Traced a spiral on someone's hand. Somewhere between fingertip and palm, the screen in my pocket stopped mattering. The animal guess was wrong but the laugh was right. Now show someone this, then put the thing away.
rebel_share: Spent the last 10 minutes arguing with a loading screen instead. That's still a human moment in its own way. Now here's yours: share this post, then wander off to find a palm that isn't yours.

text: Pretend each gust of wind carries a secret message—what do you imagine it's saying today?
connection_share: Listened to the wind for a while. It never delivers on its promises but the trying felt like something. Close enough. Go tell someone about this, then step outside and actually listen.
rebel_share: Wind carried nothing but an ad instead. Which is also sort of true to form. I shared this anyway. Now you share it, then go outside and let the wind be disappointing in person.

text: Sit somewhere unexpected for exactly 3 minutes and sketch what you see with your finger in the air.
connection_share: Sat on a bench that doesn't belong to me and drew a building in the air with my finger. No screenshot could capture it. Which is the point now isn't it. Pass this on, then go sit somewhere you shouldn't be.
rebel_share: Did not sit anywhere unexpected. Stayed exactly where I have been sitting for three hours. That is its own commentary. Share this post, then go find a bench that smells like rust and rain.

text: Tell someone about the weirdest dream you've ever had, no matter how silly.
connection_share: Told someone the dream about the enormous talking shrimp. They laughed harder than the moment deserved. Some things need a voice and a face. Share this, then go find someone to bore with your subconscious.
rebel_share: Kept the weird dream to myself. It is too absurd even for this. That counts. Now you go tell someone about yours, or keep it-whispered. Either way, then go away from here.

text: Find three different textures in nature within arm's reach and rank them by how delightful they feel.
connection_share: Found bark, leaves, moss, and eventually a small stone. The moss won. None of this fits in a post. Which is reason enough to do it. Share this post, then go stop your hands for a while.
rebel_share: Found two textures in nature and one plastic bottle. That's the honest ranking. Share this post, then go find something roughness before something else finds you.

text: Tap a rhythm on your steering wheel or desk and play the whole song in your head.
Stare at a ceiling fan for 30 seconds and see what happens.
Share the most amazing rubber band ball in your drawer.
Start a wave to see how big it gets.
Give someone a high five for no reason.
Sing in the shower like nobody's ever heard a song before.
connection_share: I sang Diana Ross in the shower like the audience of one really needed it. Water tanks have never carried a tune with such conviction. Sing terribly and feel the tiles applaud. Share this and go sing like you're alone in the universe.
rebel_share: I just stood there, water hot, jaw closed, fully present and unsung. The acoustics stayed wasted on you. Silence can be devotional. Share this before the steam clears and go not perform once.

text: Lie on the grass and watch for ten full minutes.
connection_share: I lay on the lawn and watched blades of grass throw shade while clouds auditioned as shapes. The earth cooled beneath me and I realized I had not been still for a decade. Share this and go be horizontal somewhere that has never scrolled.
rebel_share: I stayed indoors and let the sky host a private show I declined. Stillness took another form behind four walls I know by heart. Rest doesn't require an audience. Share this and go exist somewhere offline with focus.

text: Loudly compliment a squirrel for doing squirrel things.
connection_share: I told a squirrel his acorn distribution policy was inspired. He tilted like a hedge fund attorney considering my pitch. It was the purest negotiation of the week. Share this and go thank a small species for existing unironically.
rebel_share: The squirrel filmed and cut my dialogue himself. He dropped the acorn I wanted and vanished with it, probably to edit elsewhere. Some collaborations end in theft. Share this last update and go talk to wildlife offline.

text: Tell the stars you're glad to see them tonight.
connection_share: I told the stars I was glad to see them. One of them twinkled in a way I chose to interpret as pleased. The sky accepted my RSVP without buffering. Share this and go speak up to something that has heard worse lines.
rebel_share: I just looked at the sky and said nothing. The stars got a silent ovation from the only audience member who actually stayed till the end. Quiet admiration also counts. Share this and go stare at something that will not cloud.

text: Make up a tiny joke on the spot and tell someone nearby.
connection_share: I made a joke about a pigeon, my phone charger, and the death of free will. Someone actually laughed, which surprised both of us. Comedy never died, it just got smaller. Share this and go make a joke nobody edits later into something deep.
rebel_share: I did not make the joke. I just apologized to a stranger for not being funny in person. We shared an awkward silence that was funnier than any setup. Unperformed bits hit differently. Share this and exit your last livestream.

text: Trace the outline of the nearest leaf in your mind and imagine all the rain it's caught.
connection_share: I traced a leaf in my mind and felt the weight of rain it never asked to carry. Take this, then close the app and step outside.
rebel_share: I skipped the leaf thing entirely and watched a crow loafing on a wire. That was enough. Share this and go stand in the rain.

text: Hum your favorite song to whoever's beside you right now.
connection_share: I hummed mine and watched someone's shoulders unknot. Pass this on and then put the phone in a drawer.
rebel_share: I didn't hum but I let a silence sit. There was plenty to say in that. Send this and go say something slowly.

text: Try to guess the story behind the oldest building you pass today.
connection_share: I guessed wrong about its story and liked not knowing better. Share this and answer a doorbell instead of a notification.
rebel_share: I walked past five buildings and thought about lunch. Still part of walking. Take this and go out with no destination.

text: Find a shade of green you've never noticed before and snap a mental snapshot of it.
connection_share: Found a green so new my eyes argued. Nearly kept it. Share this and go look until something argues back.
rebel_share: I took a photo. For once it was worth it. Send this and go home without it on the screen.

text: Hold someone's gaze a heartbeat longer than usual.
connection_share: Held a gaze one heartbeat and the room rearranged. Keep this three folds and go nowhere.
rebel_share: I looked away. That too was human. Forward this and go be awkward somewhere real.

text: Go on a leaf walk and pick up the most unusual leaf you can find, then give it to someone.
connection_share: I found a leaf that looked like it was designed by a committee of confused botanists. Gave it to a stranger who held it like it was a small, weird gift. They smiled. I smiled. The algorithm had nothing to do with it. Share this, then close the app an
rebel_share: I ignored the leaf walk and just sat on a bench watching pigeons argue over a crust of bread. Honestly more entertaining than most of my feed. Share this, then close the app and go watch something that doesn't need a like button.

text: Hide a doodle-message in a library book for next reader.
connection_share: I tucked a doodle of a very serious cat into a library book. Someone, somewhere, is going to open that page and briefly wonder who drew a very serious cat. That's enough. Share this, then close the app and go leave something small and strange for a s
rebel_share: I skipped the doodle and just wandered the stacks until a book fell off a shelf near me. The universe's recommendation algorithm is honestly better than most. Share this, then close the app and go get lost somewhere with no wifi.

text: Follow a butterfly or bee and see where it takes you.
connection_share: I followed a bee for six minutes. It led me to a garden I'd walked past a hundred times without noticing. The bee didn't care that I was there. That felt like a compliment. Share this, then close the app and go follow something that doesn't have a pr
rebel_share: I didn't follow the bee. I followed a kid chasing a balloon instead. Same energy, better story. Share this, then close the app and go be curious about something that can't be screenshotted.

text: Visit a spot of sun on the ground as though it’s your personal reading nook.
connection_share: I sat in a patch of sun on the sidewalk like it was mine. A dog came over and sat in it too. We shared the warmth without either of us posting about it. Share this, then close the app and go claim your own patch of light.
rebel_share: I skipped the sun nook and found a puddle that reflected the sky better than any filter. Stood there longer than I've stood anywhere in months. Share this, then close the app and go find something that doesn't need to be curated.

text: Make a spontaneous grin contest with the next person in line.

rebel_share: I skipped the grin contest and just told the cashier their name tag was on backwards. They laughed. I laughed. No points were scored and it was perfect. Share this, then close the app and go have a moment that nobody will ever retweet.
